claim: For all integers n, n^2 + n is even.
For all integers n, n(n+1) is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^2 and n have the same parity.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 42.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 5.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 7.
For all integers n, gcd(n,n+1)=1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2+n,n)=abs(n).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2+n,n+1)=abs(n+1).
For all integers n, one of n and n+1 is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, n(n+1)(2n+1) is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, the sum 1^2+...+n^2 is an integer multiple of n(n+1)(2n+1)/6.
For all odd integers n, n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 8.
For all odd integers n, n^2+n is divisible by 2 but not by 4.
For all odd integers n, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, n^8 is congruent to 1 modulo 32.
For all integers n, n^3+n is even.
For all integers n, n^5+n is even.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2+n,2)=2 if and only if n^2+n is nonzero.
For all integers n, n(n-1)(n+1) is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n(n-1)(n+1) is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^3-n has at least four divisors whenever it is nonzero.
For all primes n, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 5.
For all primes n greater than 5, n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 24.
For all primes n greater than 3, n^2-1 is divisible by 24.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2-1,n)=1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2-1,n+1)=abs(n+1) except when n=-1.
For all integers n, phi(abs(n*(n+1))+1) is even whenever abs(n(n+1))+1 exceeds 2.
For all integers n, sigma(abs(n*(n+1))+1) is at least phi(abs(n*(n+1))+1).
For all integers n, gcd(n^2+n,n^2-n)=2|n| whenever n is nonzero.
For all integers n, n divides gcd(n^2+n,n^2-n).
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by 24 whenever n is odd.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^3-n,3) is at least 1 whenever n is not divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^5-n is divisible by n^3-n.
For all integers n, (n^5-n)/(n^3-n) is an integer whenever n^3-n is nonzero.
For all integers n, n^3-n factors into three consecutive integers.
For all integers n, tau(abs(n^3-n)) is even whenever n^3-n is nonzero and not a square.
For all primes n greater than 3, n is congruent to 1 or 5 modulo 6.
For all primes n greater than 3, n^2+n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^2+n+2 is even.
For all integers n, if n^2+n+2 is prime then it is equal to 2 or an odd prime.
For all odd integers n, n^2+n has exactly one factor of 2 on average structurally.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^2+n,2)=1.
For all integers n, phi(abs(n^2+n)+2) is even whenever abs(n^2+n)+2 exceeds 2.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2+n+1,n)=1.
For all integers n, gcd(n^2+n+1,n+1)=1.
For all integers n, if n^2+n+1 is prime then phi(n^2+n+1)=n^2+n.
For all integers n, n^5-n is divisible by 10.
For all integers n, the last digit of n^5 equals the last digit of n.
For all integers n, n^7-n is divisible by 14.
For all integers n, among n-1, n, n+1 at least one is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, (n-1)n(n+1) is divisible by 24 whenever n is odd.
